name: GRC Framework Ontology (Governance, Risk & Compliance)
description: Governance-centric hub ontology for the GRC series. 15 owned entities covering governance frameworks, bodies, decision rights, policies, assurance, findings, governance codes, principles, board-level actions, glossary terms, cross-framework mappings, regulatory requirements, and compliance frameworks. Aligned to COBIT 2019, ISO 38500:2024, NIST CSF 2.0, IIA Three Lines Model 2020, NCSC Cyber Governance Code 2025. 4-level governance model (Platform/Instance/Product/Client). 48 relationships (24 internal + 24 cross-ontology). 7 VSEM bridges to VE-Series. Cross-domain edges to ERM, COMP-FW, SEC-FW, AI-GOV.

components.GRCContext: Top-level GRC profile with governance level, compliance posture, sector classification
components.GovernanceFramework: Adopted governance framework — ISO 38500 EDM cycle, COBIT EDM objectives, NIST CSF GOVERN
components.GovernanceBody: Governance body — boards, committees, IIA Three Lines role assignment
components.DecisionRight: Formalised decision authority with RACI, thresholds, and 11 decision domains
components.GovernanceAssurance: Assurance activity — 8 types inc. PenTest, RedTeam, AI-Model-Audit
components.AssuranceFinding: Finding from assurance with severity, status, remediation lifecycle
components.RegulatoryRequirement: Regulation with enforcement body, jurisdiction, AI relevance classification
components.GovernanceModel: Corporate governance structure — board composition, committees, policy approach
components.GovernanceCode: Published governance code with principles and board-level actions
components.GovernancePrinciple: High-level governance principle within a code
components.GovernanceAction: Board-level governance action with dependency chains
components.GovernanceGlossaryTerm: Defined term from a governance code glossary
components.GovernanceActionMapping: Cross-framework mapping to external standard controls

crossOntology.name: complianceRequiresITMaturity
crossOntology.purpose: GRC-FW → CTX: Compliance requires IT maturity

crossOntology.name: constrainsExecution
crossOntology.purpose: GRC-FW → VSOM: VSEM governance guardrails on execution

crossOntology.name: validatesAlignment
crossOntology.purpose: GRC-FW → VSOM: VSEM assurance validates strategy

crossOntology.name: achievesObjective
crossOntology.purpose: GRC-FW → VSOM: VSEM body achieves strategic objectives

crossOntology.name: requiresAlignment
crossOntology.purpose: GRC-FW → VSOM: VSEM decision requires strategic alignment

crossOntology.name: measuresImpact
crossOntology.purpose: GRC-FW → VSOM: VSEM finding measures strategic impact

versionHistory.changes: RCSG→GRC morph. Namespace grc-fw. 6 new entities (GovernanceFramework, GovernanceBody, DecisionRight, GovernancePolicy, GovernanceAssurance, AssuranceFinding). 8 new enumerations. 24 cross-ontology relationships inc. cross-domain (ERM, SEC-FW, COMP-FW, AI-GOV) and 7 VSEM bridges (VSOM). 7 new business rules. 4-level governance model. Security mapping: governsSecurity, policyAppliesToSecurityDomain, assuranceAssessesSecurityPosture. 15 owned entities, 48 total relationships (24 internal + 24 cross-ontology), 12 enumerations, 17 business rules, 11 join patterns. Epic 30 F30.1.
Merged CG-ONT into RCSG-FW. 9 entities, 24 relationships, 10 business rules, 4 enumerations. 150 cross-framework mappings. Epic 30 F30.11.
Initial release: 4 entities, 10 relationships, 4 business rules.

notes: GRC-FW v3.0.0 is the governance-centric hub for the GRC ontology series. Evolved from RCSG-FW v2.0.0. Governance is superordinate — risk, compliance, security, AI governance operate as governed domains. 48 relationships (24 internal + 24 cross-ontology). 7 VSEM bridges: alignsToVision, supportsStrategy, constrainsExecution, validatesAlignment, achievesObjective, requiresAlignment, measuresImpact. 150 cross-framework mapping instances across 9 international standards preserved from v2.0.0. Security-specific edges: governsSecurity, policyAppliesToSecurityDomain, assuranceAssessesSecurityPosture.

oaa:joinPatterns.name: GRC Framework to CTX Jurisdiction Bridge
Governance Framework to Body to Code Hierarchy
oaa:joinPatterns.joinPath: grc-fw:GovernanceFramework → hasGovernanceBody → GovernanceBody → bodyOwnsCode → GovernanceCode → hasPrinciple → GovernancePrinciple → hasAction → GovernanceAction
